anchor: PDF p. 1 title block and current arXiv metadata
supported_content: Stochastic Path Sampler For Lattice Field Theory
Sample an unnormalized lattice target near criticality without target training data while ordinary Markov chains have long autocorrelations.

anchor: PDF pp. 1 and 3-8, abstract and Sec. 2.
supported_content: Learn forward and auxiliary backward Langevin paths by a path-space variational free-energy or entropy-production objective, then correct independent trajectory proposals with extended-space IMH.
For the reported 2D phi4 tests, corrected SPS agrees with HMC observables and reports a shorter autocorrelation in the critical region.
The autocorrelation units differ between IMH steps and HMC trajectories, so the paper does not establish a cost-matched total-efficiency comparison.

anchor: PDF p. 1 title block and current arXiv metadata
supported_content: Flow-based generative models for Markov chain Monte Carlo in lattice field theory

anchor: PDF p. 1 abstract and Sec. I.
supported_content: Reduce critical slowing in lattice-field Markov-chain sampling.
Train a normalizing-flow proposal and use an independence Metropolis-Hastings chain to correct it to the target distribution.

anchor: PDF p. 1 abstract and Sec. III.
supported_content: Training improves the reported autocorrelation behavior and the paper compares with HMC and local Metropolis for 2D phi4.

anchor: PDF p. 1, end of Sec. I.
supported_content: The concrete numerical demonstration is 2D phi4; higher-dimensional and QCD scaling are explicitly future-development questions.

anchor: PDF p. 1 title block and current arXiv metadata
supported_content: Equivariant flow-based sampling for lattice gauge theory
Construct learned samplers that respect lattice-gauge symmetry and improve topological sampling near criticality.
Use gauge-invariant-by-construction, invertible flow-based sampling algorithms.
In 2D U(1), the reported approach is orders of magnitude more efficient for topological quantities near critical points than HMC and Heat Bath.
The stated demonstration is 2D U(1), so it does not by itself prove performance for general gauge groups or QCD.

anchor: PDF p. 1 title block and current arXiv metadata
supported_content: Path Integral Sampler: a stochastic control approach for sampling
Sample unnormalized densities through a controlled stochastic path rather than an explicit terminal density model.

anchor: PDF p. 1 abstract and p. 2 Sec. 1.
supported_content: Cast a Schrodinger bridge as stochastic optimal control and use Girsanov-based importance weights to compensate suboptimal control and time discretization.
The paper gives a Wasserstein-distance justification for suboptimal control and reports comparative experiments on several sampling tasks.

anchor: PDF p. 1 abstract and p. 2 Sec. 1.
supported_content: This is a general-sampling paper rather than a lattice-field benchmark, so its relation to SPS is methodological rather than empirical equivalence.

anchor: PDF p. 1 title block and current arXiv metadata
supported_content: Stochastic normalizing flows as non-equilibrium transformations
Connect generative sampling with nonequilibrium free-energy estimation in lattice field theory.
Combine neural normalizing-flow layers with Monte Carlo updates and identify the framework with Jarzynski-based nonequilibrium transformations.
The article lays out an efficiency-optimization strategy and applications for this extended generative-model class.
It establishes a conceptual and algorithmic bridge, not a direct SPS-versus-baseline performance comparison.

anchor: PDF p. 1 title block and current arXiv metadata
supported_content: Stochastic normalizing flows for lattice field theory
Describe hybrid stochastic normalizing flows for sampling Boltzmann distributions in lattice field theory.
Combine normalizing flows with Monte Carlo updates in a framework related to Jarzynski equality and nonequilibrium statistical mechanics.

anchor: PDF p. 1 abstract and p. 2 Sec. 2.
supported_content: The proceeding presents construction details and 2D phi4 applications.
This is a short proceeding and should be used as an implementation account, with detailed claims checked against the underlying article.

anchor: PDF p. 1 title block and current arXiv metadata
supported_content: Denoising Diffusion Samplers
Sample approximately from unnormalized targets and estimate their normalizing constants through diffusion-style paths.
Approximate the reverse of a target-to-Gaussian diffusion and optimize an extended KL or ELBO using an auxiliary stationary reference process.

anchor: PDF p. 1 abstract and p. 2 Sec. 1.
supported_content: The paper gives theoretical guarantees discussed for DDS and compares sampling methods on challenging non-lattice tasks.
DDS is an adjacent unnormalized-target method, not a lattice-field result in this paper.

anchor: PDF p. 1 title block and current arXiv metadata
supported_content: Diffusion Models as Stochastic Quantization in Lattice Field Theory
Relate generative diffusion models to stochastic quantization and test them as global lattice samplers.
Approximate reverse Langevin stochastic dynamics to move from a prior toward the lattice target distribution.
The reported 2D phi4 diffusion sampler reduces autocorrelation in the critical region.
The claim is tied to the tested 2D phi4 setup and the reported model configuration.

anchor: PDF p. 1 title block and current arXiv metadata
supported_content: Out-of-equilibrium simulations to fight topological freezing

anchor: PDF p. 1 abstract and p. 2 Sec. 1.
supported_content: Mitigate long topological autocorrelations in lattice gauge simulations.
Use out-of-equilibrium evolutions with open-boundary starting configurations, gradually restore periodic boundaries, and apply a Jarzynski-inspired reweighting-like correction.

anchor: PDF p. 1 abstract and p. 2 Sec. 1.
supported_content: The contribution reports tests in 2D CP(N-1) and a comparison with parallel tempering on boundary conditions.
It is a conference contribution on CP(N-1), and its link to SPS is a shared nonequilibrium family rather than a verified direct root citation.

anchor: PDF p. 1 title block and current arXiv metadata
supported_content: Mitigating topological freezing using out-of-equilibrium simulations
Mitigate topological freezing using out-of-equilibrium lattice Monte Carlo simulations.
Use a Jarzynski-motivated out-of-equilibrium protocol and assess its ESS, KL, topological susceptibility, autocorrelation, and efficiency.
For 2D CP(N-1), the reported performance is comparable to the cited parallel-tempering-on-boundary-conditions baseline.
The article presents a non-ML protocol and says machine-learning combinations are future work; it is not evidence that an SNF or SPS has been tested there.

anchor: PDF p. 1 title block and current arXiv metadata
supported_content: Variational Autoregressive Networks Applied to $φ^4$ Field Theory Systems
Train and sample discrete Ising and continuous phi4 models without reference data.

anchor: PDF p. 1 abstract and contents Sec. 2.4.
supported_content: Combine reinforcement learning with variational autoregressive networks and add single-site or block Metropolis-Hastings corrections to VAN proposals.
For the explored ranges, MH corrections reduce residual bias, results agree with standard Monte Carlo within errors, and no clear critical slowing down is observed.
The evidence is for the reported Ising and phi4 settings and uses ESS alongside corrections; it does not itself establish a universal wall-time win.

anchor: PDF p. 1 title block and current arXiv metadata
supported_content: Scalable Generative Sampling and Multilevel Estimation for Lattice Field Theories Near Criticality

anchor: PDF p. 1 abstract and Sec. I.
supported_content: Address critical slowing down and scaling limits of single-scale learned lattice samplers near criticality.
Use a coarse-to-fine multiscale generative sampler with conditional Gaussian mixtures, masked continuous normalizing flows, and exact restriction maps for MLMC.
For reported critical 2D phi4 studies, the method has much shorter integrated autocorrelation than HMC, high importance-sampling efficiency, and observables agreeing with long HMC simulations.
The reported numerical claim is a 2D critical-phi4 result and must not be recast as a general cost-normalized theorem.

anchor: PDF p. 1 title block and current arXiv metadata
supported_content: Operator Spectroscopy of Trained Lattice Samplers

anchor: PDF p. 1 summary and Sec. I.
supported_content: Expose what trained lattice samplers represent internally rather than judging them only by output ensembles.
Project a trained velocity, score, or learned-action residual onto symmetry-fixed operator dictionaries and retain sectors only when held-out and control tests support them.
The paper reports separable zero-mode and finite-momentum residual sectors in 2D phi4 and analogous diagnostics for diffusion, normalizing-flow, and gauge-equivariant teachers.

anchor: PDF p. 1 summary and Sec. I.
supported_content: Operator spectroscopy is a diagnostic of learned functions; it is not by itself a sampling-efficiency benchmark.

anchor: PDF p. 1 title block and current arXiv metadata
supported_content: Diffusion Models for Sampling Near Criticality in Lattice Field Theories

anchor: PDF p. 1 abstract and p. 2 Sec. I.
supported_content: Assess diffusion sampling for 2D and 3D phi4 across phases and test cross-volume transfer near criticality.
Train score-based reverse-SDE samplers and evaluate local score checks, MALA acceptance, HMC-referenced ESS, observable bias and variance, and cross-volume transfer.
The reported model reproduces many scalar observables and propagators; residual bias remains in the zero mode and in 3D action density, with one reported broken-phase susceptibility exception.
This is a July 2026 forward neighbor of SPS, and its explicit residuals show why a high summary metric alone is insufficient.
